Title: Investigating the Impact of Electrical Stimulation on Facial Pain, Jaw Movement and Oral Health in People With Motor Neuron Disease.
Criteria_Raw:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Diagnosis of possible, probable, or definite ALS (El-Escorial Revisited) OR diagnosis of definite PLS by the treating neurologist and based on the consensus diagnostic criteria for PLS
* Subjective report of jaw pain, indicated by a NRS score of ≥ or = 3/10.

Exclusion Criteria:

* history of head or neck cancer
* history of CVA
* history of past facial surgery with hardware placement
* history of pacemaker
* diagnosis of significant cognitive impairment by the treating p...
Screening_Q0: Here are the 4 yes/no screening questions:
Screening_Q1: Have you been diagnosed with ALS (Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis) or PLS (Primary Lateral Sclerosis)?
Screening_Q3: Have you ever had head or neck cancer?

Title: Home Versus Hospital Based NIV Care in MND
Criteria_Raw:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Adults with a diagnosis of Motor Neurone Disease confirmed by a neurologist specialising in MND.
* Indications to NIV: daytime pCO2 ≥6.0 kPa or/and symptoms of ventilatory failure (e.g. orthopnoea, paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnoea, morning drowsiness) in the presence of significant diaphragmatic weakness confirmed by respiratory muscle tests

Exclusion Criteria:

* Cognitive impairment precluding understanding of the study protocol and valid consent
* Severe co-morbidities (...
Screening_Q0: Here are the 4 simple yes/no screening questions:
Screening_Q2: Do you have a confirmed diagnosis of Motor Neurone Disease from a neurologist?
Screening_Q3: Have you experienced symptoms such as orthopnoea, paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnoea, or morning drowsiness due to ventilatory failure?

Title: Multicenter ALS Imaging Study
Criteria_Raw:
For participants with ALS:

* \< 36 months since onset of symptoms
* Definite, probable, lab supported-probable or possible ALS by El Escorial criteria OR definite, probable or possible ALS per Awaji-Shima Criteria
* Forced vital capacity within the last 90 days ≥ 60% of the predicted value
* Able to consent for themselves
* Able to read and speak English
* Clear of any contraindications for MRI

Exclusion Criteria:

* Individuals will be excluded if they have any condition that makes MRI unsafe...
Screening_Q0: Here are the 4 simple yes/no screening questions:
Screening_Q1: 1. Have you had ALS symptoms for less than 36 months?
Screening_Q2: 2. Do you meet El Escorial criteria or Awaji-Shima criteria for definite, probable, lab supported-probable, or possible ALS?
Screening_Q3: 3. Is your forced vital capacity within the last 90 days at least 60% of predicted value?

Title: Controlling Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis Motor Neuron Excitability Study
Criteria_Raw:
Inclusion Criteria:

1. 18-80 years of age inclusive
2. Sporadic or familial ALS diagnosed as clinically possible, probable, lab-supported probable, or definite ALS as defined by revised El Escorial criteria
3. Less than or equal to 3 years since ALS symptom onset
4. Slow Vital Capacity ≥ 50% of predicted capacity at the time of Screening as determined using a portable spirometer
5. For TTNCS: Median CMAP ≥ 1.5 mV
6. Willing to forgo botulinum toxin, phenol or alcohol injections, intrathecal bac...
Screening_Q0: Here are the 4 simple yes/no screening questions:
Screening_Q1: Are you between 18 and 80 years old?
Screening_Q2: Have you been diagnosed with ALS using revised El Escorial criteria?
Screening_Q3: Has it been less than or equal to 3 years since your ALS symptoms started?

Title: Spinal Cord Associative Plasticity for ALS
Criteria_Raw:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Diagnosis of ALS by Gold Coast Criteria (Shefner et al. 2020) or "definite" or "probable" ALS by revised El Escorial Criteria (Brooks et al. 2000)
* Incomplete weakness: Score of 1, 2, 3, or 4 (out of 5) on manual muscle testing of finger extension, finger flexion, or finger abduction in left or right hand
* TSCS-evoked potential amplitude of at least 25 V in left or right abductor pollicis brevis (APB) or first dorsal interosseous (FDI) muscles with a resting motor thresh...
Screening_Q0: Here are the 4 simple yes/no screening questions:
Screening_Q1: Have you been diagnosed with ALS?
Screening_Q2: Do you have weakness in your hands or fingers?
Screening_Q3: Has your doctor used manual muscle testing to assess your hand function?

Title: Hypercaloric PEG Nutrition in ALS to Sustain Energy Homeostasis
Criteria_Raw:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Possible, probable (clinically or laboratory) or definite ALS according to the revised version of the El Escorial World Federation of Neurology criteria 1
* Loss of ALS functional rating scale revised (ALSFRS-R) of ≥ 0.33 points per month since onset (first paresis) based on the formula: (48 - Score at Screening Visit) / (Months between Onset and Screening Visit)
* Nutrition via PEG
* Age ≥18 years
* Intake of a stable dose of riluzole for at least 4 weeks, or no riluzole
...
Screening_Q0: Here are the 4 simple yes/no screening questions:
Screening_Q1: 1. Have you been diagnosed with ALS according to the revised El Escorial World Federation of Neurology criteria?
Screening_Q2: 2. Has your ALSFRS-R score decreased by at least 0.33 points per month since your symptoms started?
Screening_Q3: 3. Do you receive nutrition via PEG (percutaneous endoscopic gastrostomy)?

Title: Oral Intake of Enteral Nutrition Formula Preceding Placement and Feeding Via GTube and Its Impact on Formula Intolerance in pALS
Criteria_Raw:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Diagnosis of possible, probable, or definite ALS or motor neuron disease by the treating neurologist (El-Escorial Revisited).
* Planned tube feeding placement within the following 4 to 6 weeks.

Exclusion Criteria:

* History of Crohns disease, inflammatory bowel disease, irritable bowel syndrome, celiac disease, food allergies and/or sensitivities to any of the formula ingredients, neurodegenerative disease diagnosis outside of ALS, or other concomitant disorder that migh...
Screening_Q0: Here are the 4 simple yes/no screening questions:
Screening_Q1: 1. Have you been diagnosed with ALS or motor neuron disease by your treating neurologist?
Screening_Q2: 2. Do you have a planned tube feeding placement within the next 6 weeks?
Screening_Q3: 3. Have you ever had Crohn's disease, inflammatory bowel disease, irritable bowel syndrome, celiac disease, or food allergies?
